0.cultural_biological: Release Trichogramma wasps, avoid excessive nitrogenous fertilizers, and clear weeds around the field edges.
0.chemical_direct: Apply Chlorantraniliprole, Flubendiamide, or Chlorpyrifos at early larval stages.

1.cultural_biological: Utilize frequency oscillation disease-killing lamps to capture adult moths, and encourage natural enemies like spiders and frogs.
1.chemical_direct: Spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) wettable powder or Indoxacarb during peak hatching.

2.cultural_biological: Drain fields periodically during peak egg-laying windows to suppress larval emergence.
2.chemical_direct: Apply systemic granular insecticides such as Thiamethoxam or Fipronil to the soil.

3.cultural_biological: Burn or plow down stubble post-harvest to destroy overwintering larvae.
3.chemical_direct: Spray Chlorantraniliprole or apply Cartap Hydrochloride granules in irrigation water.

4.cultural_biological: Harvest crops close to the ground level and submerge stubble with water to kill pupae.
4.chemical_direct: Use sex pheromone traps for mass trapping or apply targeted sprays of Emamectin Benzoate.

5.cultural_biological: Use gall midge-resistant cultivars and conserve platygasterid parasite wasps.
5.chemical_direct: Broadcast Carbofuran or Fipronil granules at the early tillering stage.

6.cultural_biological: Adjust planting dates to avoid early-season fly peaks and manually clear alternate grass hosts.
6.chemical_direct: Apply systemic foliar sprays like Dimethoate or Imidacloprid.

7.cultural_biological: Practice alternate wetting and drying (AWD) water management and avoid dense planting.
7.chemical_direct: Spray Pymetrozine, Dinotefuran, or Triflumezopyrim directed at the base of the plants.

8.cultural_biological: Maintain wide spacing between rows and encourage predatory mirid bugs.
8.chemical_direct: Apply Buprofezin or Thiamethoxam when populations cross economic thresholds.

9.cultural_biological: Clean field bunds to destroy overwintering environments; monitor for rice stripe virus transmission.
9.chemical_direct: Apply Imidacloprid or Clothianidin during the early vegetative phase.

10.cultural_biological: Delayed flooding of fields where possible and clear aquatic weeds.
10.chemical_direct: Apply seed treatments with Cyantraniliprole or apply Lambda-Cyhalothrin to foliage.

11.cultural_biological: Use yellow sticky traps and prune overlapping dense canopies to lower humidity.
11.chemical_direct: Apply Acetamiprid or Buprofezin sprays early in the morning.

12.cultural_biological: Maintain a continuous thin layer of water in the field to drown thrips nymphs.
12.chemical_direct: Spray Spinetoram or Azadirachtin (Neem-based formulation) upon first detection.

13.cultural_biological: Manually collect visible egg masses and adults; optimize deep plowing during field prep.
13.chemical_direct: Apply contact insecticides like Cypermethrin or Abamectin if outbreaks occur.

14.cultural_biological: Deep plow the soil during autumn or winter to expose grubs to birds and cold weather.
14.chemical_direct: Apply Metarhizium anisopliae bio-insecticide or mix Chlorpyrifos granules into the soil.

15.cultural_biological: Flood fields to force crickets to the surface for manual collection or bird predation.
15.chemical_direct: Deploy toxic baits mixed with Trichlorfon or apply Imidacloprid into tunnels.

16.cultural_biological: Implement intensive crop rotation with non-host plants and optimize summer fallow tilling.
16.chemical_direct: Use Bifenthrin or Clothianidin as a seed coat or pre-planting soil treatment.

17.cultural_biological: Deploy light traps to catch adult moths and manually crush egg clusters.
17.chemical_direct: Spray Lambda-Cyhalothrin or Chlorantraniliprole during larval outbreaks.

18.cultural_biological: Remove weeds 10-14 days before planting to starve young larvae.
18.chemical_direct: Apply synthetic pyrethroid sprays (e.g., Deltamethrin) or bran baits late in the evening.

19.cultural_biological: Utilize physical barriers around high-value seedlings and implement deep mechanical tilling.
19.chemical_direct: Apply targeted applications of Indoxacarb or Chlorfenapyr to the base of plants.

20.cultural_biological: Keep field boundaries completely clean of host weeds; utilize structural row covers.
20.chemical_direct: Deploy molasses-pesticide baits or spray Emamectin Benzoate around affected clusters.

21.cultural_biological: Introduce predatory mites (Phytoseiidae) and maintain overhead sprinkler irrigation to wash them away.
21.chemical_direct: Spray specific acaricides like Abamectin, Spirodiclofen, or Hexythiazox.

22.cultural_biological: Plant resistant hybrids and release Trichogramma ostriniae wasps at initial egg laying.
22.chemical_direct: Apply Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) granules or spray Chlorantraniliprole into the whorls.

23.cultural_biological: Dig deep defensive trenches around fields to stop migrating crawls; protect bird populations.
23.chemical_direct: Spray Methomyl, Flubendiamide, or Spinosad evenly across the crop canopy.

24.cultural_biological: Conserve ladybug beetles and lacewings; blast small infestations with high-pressure water.
24.chemical_direct: Apply Imidacloprid, Thiamethoxam, or Flonicamid systemic sprays.

25.cultural_biological: Hang sweet-and-sour liquid bait traps (sugar, vinegar, wine mix) to capture adult beetles.
25.chemical_direct: Apply targeted contact treatments like Carbaryl or Cypermethrin to aggregate sites.

26.cultural_biological: Apply mating disruption pheromone dispensers across orchards early in the season.
26.chemical_direct: Paint or spray the lower trunk sections with Chlorpyrifos or Permethrin.

27.cultural_biological: Incorporate intercropping methods and protect natural parasitoid wasps (Aphidius spp.).
27.chemical_direct: Spray Pirimicarb or Dimethoate if pest populations cross economic thresholds.

28.cultural_biological: Grow bug-resistant grain varieties and balance fertilizer application rates.
28.chemical_direct: Apply systemic options like Acetamiprid or Pymetrozine.

29.cultural_biological: Destroy winter host trees near production fields; encourage lady beetle propagation.
29.chemical_direct: Apply seed treatments with Imidacloprid or foliar sprays of Thiamethoxam.

30.cultural_biological: Utilize resistant wheat varieties and implement crop rotations out of grass species.
30.chemical_direct: Spray Lambda-Cyhalothrin exactly at heading when midges emerge at dusk.

31.cultural_biological: Rotate fields with non-susceptible crops like lentils; optimize summer fallowing.
31.chemical_direct: Apply Bifenthrin or Omethoate during early autumn or winter when mites hatch.

32.cultural_biological: Increase dust-control practices along field borders and provide adequate watering.
32.chemical_direct: Apply targeted miticides such as Bifenazate or Pyridaben.

33.cultural_biological: Burn or plow deep into crop residues immediately after harvest to disrupt life cycles.
33.chemical_direct: Apply systemic foliar insecticides like Malathion or Imidacloprid at early boot stage.

34.cultural_biological: Implement deep autumn plowing to bury pupae and rotate out of small grains.
34.chemical_direct: Apply contact pyrethroids such as Alpha-Cypermethrin prior to larval boring.

35.cultural_biological: Remove wild host grasses around wheat fields and cut forage crops early.
35.chemical_direct: Spray Dimethoate or other systemic organophosphates to target mining larvae.

36.cultural_biological: Use anti-insect nets over seedbeds and apply crop rotation systems.
36.chemical_direct: Apply Deltamethrin or Acetamiprid upon finding leaf-mining tunnels.

37.cultural_biological: Incorporate trap crops like radish or mustard; use row covers on young crops.
37.chemical_direct: Dust or spray with Carbaryl, Bifenthrin, or Spinosad.

38.cultural_biological: Manually collect and destroy large egg clusters; apply Bacillus thuringiensis strain kurstaki.
38.chemical_direct: Spray Chlorantraniliprole, Emamectin Benzoate, or Indoxacarb.

39.cultural_biological: Remove pigweed and alternate host weeds; release green lacewings.
39.chemical_direct: Apply Spinetoram, Chlorfenapyr, or Lufenuron to prevent chitin synthesis.

40.cultural_biological: Utilize yellow sticky boards for population monitoring and adult removal.
40.chemical_direct: Apply targeted systemic sprays of Cyromazine or Imidacloprid.

41.cultural_biological: Deep till soil in autumn to crush overwintering cocoons; mow weed boundaries.
41.chemical_direct: Apply contact pyrethroids like Lambda-Cyhalothrin during early caterpillar stages.

42.cultural_biological: Dig trapping ditches around new beet fields adjacent to old beet locations.
42.chemical_direct: Apply Phosmet or Alpha-Cypermethrin early in the spring to target adult beetles.

43.cultural_biological: Use black light traps to collect adult beetles at night during summer months.
43.chemical_direct: Drench soil with Imidacloprid or apply Bifenthrin granules to target root-feeding larvae.

44.cultural_biological: Harvest the first alfalfa crop early to eliminate eggs and young larvae mechanically.
44.chemical_direct: Spray Indoxacarb, Flubendiamide, or Lambda-Cyhalothrin if thresholds are exceeded.

45.cultural_biological: Optimize early planting schedules to ensure flowering occurs before peak pest populations emerge.
45.chemical_direct: Apply Methomyl or Spinosad when buds show initial feeding damage.

46.cultural_biological: Cut alfalfa fields cleanly to remove egg-laying sites in the stems.
46.chemical_direct: Apply targeted foliar sprays of Dimethoate or Cypermethrin.

47.cultural_biological: Control broadleaf weeds in and around fields; maintain clear buffers.
47.chemical_direct: Apply Flonicamid, Sulfoxaflor, or Bifenthrin during early nymph phases.

48.cultural_biological: Protect natural bird habitats; implement Nosema locustae biological bait treatments.
48.chemical_direct: Apply broad-scale applications of Malathion, Carbaryl, or Diflubenzuron.

49.cultural_biological: Hand-pick beetles carefully using protective gloves (due to cantharidin blisters).
49.chemical_direct: Spray Methomyl or Carbaryl along field edges where swarms concentrate.

50.cultural_biological: Maintain weed-free conservation strips and timing alfalfa cuttings to minimize blooming.
50.chemical_direct: Apply targeted foliar treatments with Cyfluthrin or Permethrin.

51.cultural_biological: Encourage natural landscape diversity; monitor hay fields closely before cutting.
51.chemical_direct: Apply Spot treatments of Pyrethrins or Carbaryl to heavy clusters.

52.cultural_biological: Introduce spotted alfalfa aphid-resistant cultivars; preserve predatory lady beetles.
52.chemical_direct: Apply Flonicamid or Chlorpyrifos if honeydew accumulation becomes severe.

53.cultural_biological: Implement standard overhead irrigation to disturb breeding cycles on legume crops.
53.chemical_direct: Spray Spinetoram or Acetamiprid combined with a surfactant.

54.cultural_biological: Deploy bright blue or yellow sticky traps; utilize silver reflective mulches.
54.chemical_direct: Apply Spinosad, Abamectin, or Azadirachtin alternated to prevent resistance.

55.cultural_biological: Destroy volunteer alfalfa plants and recycle or burn infested seed screenings.
55.chemical_direct: No effective chemical rescue exists for internal seed larvae; apply contact pyrethroids to adults pre-bloom.

56.cultural_biological: Manually clean off visible green caterpillars; use fine insect netting exclusions.
56.chemical_direct: Apply Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) or Spinosad sprays regularly.

57.cultural_biological: Plant green bean trap crops around main plots; clear host weeds like wormwood.
57.chemical_direct: Spray Imidacloprid, Sulfoxaflor, or Beta-Cyfluthrin during early nymph stages.

58.cultural_biological: Prune out leaves with cocoon formations during winter; protect insectivorous birds.
58.chemical_direct: Apply Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) or Chlorantraniliprole to young larvae safely.

59.cultural_biological: Graft grapevines exclusively onto resistant rootstocks (e.g., Couderc or Richter varieties).
59.chemical_direct: Apply Spirotetramat or Imidacloprid soil drenches to suppress root infestations.

60.cultural_biological: Prune and destroy distorted blistered leaves early in spring growth.
60.chemical_direct: Apply wettable sulfur sprays at bud break or use Abamectin post-bloom.

61.cultural_biological: Preserve native phytoseiid predatory mites by avoiding broad-spectrum sprays.
61.chemical_direct: Apply Spirodiclofen, Fenpyroximate, or sulfur formulations.

62.cultural_biological: Manually collect adult beetles and larvae from grape leaves in small vineyards.
62.chemical_direct: Spray Beta-Cypermethrin or Phosmet during peak adult emergence.

63.cultural_biological: Maintain high humidity but avoid water stress; remove infected host plants.
63.chemical_direct: Apply Abamectin, Fenpyroximate, or Spiromesifen to leaf undersides.

64.cultural_biological: Release predatory ladybeetles (Cryptolaemus montrouzieri) and clear loose bark.
64.chemical_direct: Apply Spirotetramat or Buprofezin during the crawler emergence phase.

65.cultural_biological: Inject entomopathogenic nematodes (Steinernema spp.) directly into borer entry holes.
65.chemical_direct: Apply Chlorpyrifos or Permethrin to trunks during adult flight windows.

66.cultural_biological: Hand-pick large hornworm caterpillars from vines; protect pupae in soil via tilling.
66.chemical_direct: Spray Spinosad or Carbaryl if populations threaten major defoliation.

67.cultural_biological: Scrape off and destroy gray mud-like egg masses; remove preferred Tree of Heaven hosts.
67.chemical_direct: Apply systemic Dinotefuran bark sprays or contact Bifenthrin applications.

68.cultural_biological: Promptly remove and burn dead, dying, or infested structural wood and vine trunks.
68.chemical_direct: Apply protective trunk sprays of Bifenthrin or Cypermethrin before egg hatching.

69.cultural_biological: Mow weeds and grass ditch banks near main crops to remove egg-laying choices.
69.chemical_direct: Apply Acetamiprid, Thiamethoxam, or Clothianidin sprays.

70.cultural_biological: Weed continuously along borders; deploy white sticky tracking cards.
70.chemical_direct: Spray Flonicamid, Sulfoxaflor, or Oxamyl strictly according to instructions.

71.cultural_biological: Introduce Encarsia formosa parasitic wasps; use yellow sticky traps.
71.chemical_direct: Apply Spiromesifen, Buprofezin, or Potassium Salts of Fatty Acids (Insecticidal Soap).

72.cultural_biological: Remove fallen leaf litter during winter cleanups to remove overwintering shelter.
72.chemical_direct: Apply Acetamiprid or Dinotefuran early in the nymph cycle.

73.cultural_biological: Hand-pick large bird-dropping mimetic caterpillars from citrus foliage.
73.chemical_direct: Apply Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) or Spinosad safely without hitting pollinators.

74.cultural_biological: Conserve predatory mites and ladybugs; maintain proper tree irrigation.
74.chemical_direct: Spray Abamectin, Spirodiclofen, or clofentezine miticides.

75.cultural_biological: Prune trees to open up canopies; monitor rust configurations on fruit regularly.
75.chemical_direct: Apply agricultural sulfur, Fenbutatin Oxide, or Abamectin treatments.

76.cultural_biological: Introduce and protect Vedalia predatory beetles (Rodolia cardinalis) and Cryptochaetum flies.
76.chemical_direct: Apply horticultural oils or use targeted systemic Pyriproxyfen.

77.cultural_biological: Prune out highly infested branches to increase light penetration and air movement.
77.chemical_direct: Apply Mineral oil sprays during winter, or Buprofezin during crawler peaks.

78.cultural_biological: Prune affected structural twigs; spray with high-pressure water wash to dislodge scale.
78.chemical_direct: Apply Imidacloprid as a soil drench or use targeted Pyriproxyfen + oil sprays.

79.cultural_biological: Encourage Aphytis holoxanthus parasitic wasps; remove heavy target leaves.
79.chemical_direct: Spray with Chlorpyrifos or Spirotetramat combined with a crop oil adjuvant.

80.cultural_biological: Enforce strict quarantine on clean nursery stock; scrape scale crusts manually from trunks.
80.chemical_direct: Apply broad systemic drenches of Dinotefuran or seasonal mineral oils.

81.cultural_biological: Control attending honeydew ants; use botanical oil washes directly on colonies.
81.chemical_direct: Apply Profenofos, Buprofezin, or Acetamiprid into interior cluster spaces.

82.cultural_biological: Conserve Encarsia smithi parasitoid wasps; prune lower inner watersprouts.
82.chemical_direct: Apply Azadirachtin, Spirotetramat, or Dinotefuran systemic options.

83.cultural_biological: Bag individual high-value fruits early; cleanly collect and deeply bury fallen fruit.
83.chemical_direct: Deploy protein hydrolysate baits mixed with Spinosad or Malathion on trunks.

84.cultural_biological: Implement the Male Annihilation Technique (MAT) using Methyl Eugenol pheromone traps.
84.chemical_direct: Spray GF-120 SpinTor Bait or apply targeted Dimethoate cover applications.

85.cultural_biological: Collect fallen citrus fruit every 3 days and destroy them via boiling or deep burial.
85.chemical_direct: Utilize bait sprays containing Spinosad applied to non-crop border vegetation.

86.cultural_biological: Hand-pick clusters; deploy light traps and Spodoptera litura sex pheromone lures.
86.chemical_direct: Apply Chlorantraniliprole, Indoxacarb, or Emamectin Benzoate.

87.cultural_biological: Orchard sanitation; immediately drop and eliminate infested fruit vectors.
87.chemical_direct: Apply targeted contact pyrethroids or organophosphates during egg-laying flights.

88.cultural_biological: Avoid late-season nitrogen inputs to limit tender flush growth; protect Ageniaspis citricola wasps.
88.chemical_direct: Spray Spinetoram, Abamectin, or Imidacloprid strictly onto new leaf flushes.

89.cultural_biological: Conserve predatory lacewings and Lysiphlebus testaceipes parasitic wasps.
89.chemical_direct: Apply systemic Imidacloprid or contact applications of Thiamethoxam.

90.cultural_biological: Prune out dense water sprouts to reduce colonial feeding sites.
90.chemical_direct: Apply Flonicamid, Acetamiprid, or Potassium Salts of Fatty Acids.

91.cultural_biological: Maintain balanced fertilization; remove alternate host spiraea bushes nearby.
91.chemical_direct: Spray Pymetrozine or Dinotefuran upon early colony grouping.

92.cultural_biological: Intercrop with non-host crops and eliminate field weeds; use blue sticky traps.
92.chemical_direct: Apply Spinetoram, Tolfenpyrad, or Abamectin in rotational cycles.

93.cultural_biological: Turn or cultivate topsoil below plants during winter to expose midge pupae.
93.chemical_direct: Apply contact pyrethroids during early budding before adults deposit eggs.

94.cultural_biological: Prune and burn structural twigs containing embedded overwintering egg insertions.
94.chemical_direct: Apply Acetamiprid or Thiamethoxam combined with an oil-based adjuvant.

95.cultural_biological: Remove wild host brush from orchard boundaries and maintain airy branch architectures.
95.chemical_direct: Spray with Buprofezin or Bifenthrin during early nymph concentrations.

96.cultural_biological: Collect and destroy clipped leaves containing eggs from the ground daily.
96.chemical_direct: Apply Lambda-Cyhalothrin or Cypermethrin to foliage during new flush stages.

97.cultural_biological: Prune out and burn mango shoots showing early wilting or boring holes.
97.chemical_direct: Inject or spray shoots with Chlorantraniliprole or Profenofos before boring occurs.

98.cultural_biological: Prune crowded inner orchard branches to allow sunlight and lower humidity.
98.chemical_direct: Spray Imidacloprid, Thiamethoxam, or Buprofezin onto tree trunks and blossoms.

99.cultural_biological: Hook out larvae from boring tunnels manually using wire or plug holes with mud.
99.chemical_direct: Inject Dichlorvos or Chlorpyrifos solutions directly into active tunnel openings and seal them.

100.cultural_biological: Collect and destroy all fallen and infested mango fruits; clean orchard floors thoroughly.
100.chemical_direct: Apply protective sprays of Deltamethrin or Alpha-Cypermethrin during fruit-set phases.

101.cultural_biological: Deploy yellow sticky cards around crops and eliminate surrounding alternate weed hosts.
101.chemical_direct: Apply systemic foliar insecticides like Dinotefuran, Acetamiprid, or Flonicamid.
